implicit_processes.description: Creation, update, and deactivation of supplier records including banking details, payment terms, and tax classifications in Oracle Supplier Master.
Entry and validation of invoices matched against approved purchase orders, including 2-way, 3-way, or 4-way matching and hold resolution.
Entry and approval of non-PO invoices for direct expenses, services, or recurring obligations that bypass the procurement process.
Creation of debit memos for supplier overcharges and credit memos for returned goods, price adjustments, or agreed disputes.
Recording of advance payments to suppliers prior to goods or service delivery, with subsequent application against standard invoices on receipt.
Cancellation of incorrectly entered or rejected invoices, including reversal of any associated accounting entries and documentation of the cancellation reason.
Execution of payment runs including payment batch creation, approval, selection of invoice batches, bank file generation, and confirmation posting.
Cancellation of processed payments due to errors, bank rejections, or supplier disputes, restoring invoices to unpaid status for reprocessing.
Period-end accruals for uninvoiced receipts, reconciliation of AP subledger to general ledger, and closure of the AP accounting period.
Creation, update, and management of customer accounts including credit limits, payment terms, billing preferences, and contact information.
Manual creation of AR transactions including standard invoices, debit memos, and charges raised directly against customer accounts.
Recording and application of customer cash receipts against open invoices, including manual and automatic matching of payments.
Issuance of credit memos to customers for returns, billing errors, or agreed adjustments, with application against open or future invoices.
Reversal of incorrectly applied receipts or returned cheques, restoring the original invoice to open status for re-collection.
Reconciliation of AR subledger to general ledger, aging analysis review, accrual for unbilled revenue, and closure of the AR accounting period.
Import and automated or manual matching of bank statement lines against Oracle cash transactions to clear outstanding items and flag unreconciled entries.
Initiation and recording of fund transfers between internal bank accounts, including approval, settlement confirmation, and accounting entry creation.
Manual entry of bank charges, interest payments, and other external bank transactions not originating from AP or AR, directly into the cash management ledger.
Creation of new asset records from purchase orders, payables invoices, or manual entry, including assignment of asset category, location, and depreciation method.
Transfer of costs accumulated in Construction-in-Progress (CIP) to depreciable asset records upon project completion and readiness for use.
Modification of existing asset attributes including cost, useful life, depreciation method, or category to reflect revaluation or correction.
Reassignment of assets between cost centers, locations, or responsible departments, updating ownership records and depreciation allocation accordingly.
Full or partial retirement of assets through sale, disposal, or write-off, including gain/loss calculation and removal from the asset register.
Periodic physical verification of assets against the Oracle asset register, reconciliation of discrepancies, and update of location or status records.
Recognition and recording of asset impairment losses where the carrying amount exceeds recoverable value, in line with applicable accounting standards.
Execution of the depreciation run, reconciliation of the asset register to the general ledger, and closure of the FA accounting period.
Creation, review, and approval of manual journal entries for accruals, corrections, allocations, and other non-automated accounting transactions.
Review, correction, and approval of journal batches prior to final posting to the general ledger, including reversal of incorrectly posted entries.
Entry, upload, and maintenance of budget amounts by cost centre and account, with variance monitoring against actuals throughout the fiscal year.
Period-end revaluation of foreign currency balances using current exchange rates, generating unrealised gain/loss entries to reflect accurate reporting currency values.
Execution of period-end procedures including subledger reconciliation, translation, consolidation, and formal closure of the accounting period to prevent further postings.
